what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG eye-off icon using stroked paths, a diagonal slash, partial eye contours, and a center circle.
observations: The SVG uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with explicit width and height of 24.
The icon has no fill and uses stroke="currentColor" for inherited color.
Stroke styling is fixed at 1.5 width with round line caps and joins.
A diagonal path runs from the upper left to the lower right.
Two curved path elements suggest the eye outline and visibility shape.
A centered circle at 12,12 with radius 2.5 remains present under the slash.
standouts: Simple inline geometry with four drawable elements.
Eye-off meaning is conveyed by combining eye contours with a diagonal slash.
conventions: Follows common SVG icon conventions: 24px canvas, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded caps and joins.
Uses path and circle primitives rather than embedded bitmap data.
Keeps all styling inline on the root SVG element.
notes: A compact stroked SVG asset for a hidden or visibility-off state.
questions: Should the center circle remain fully drawn beneath the diagonal slash?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests an eye-off icon, and the SVG content matches that purpose by drawing an eye motif crossed by a diagonal slash.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable interface icon for hiding, disabling visibility, or indicating that content is not shown.
contribution: Contributes a compact, themeable visibility-off SVG glyph that can be retained as an icon asset for hidden-state controls or visual status indicators.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24px currentColor stroked SVG eye-off icon composed of a diagonal slash, partial eye-outline paths, and a centered circle.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a hidden, obscured, or visibility-disabled state in a UI icon set.
